title: cotula reptans var. major
intro: Cotula reptans var. major, commonly known as greater buttonweed or creeping cotula, is a perennial herb in the daisy family that grows up to 30 centimeters tall and has yellow flowers and button-like leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cotula reptans var. major include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve digestion, and even exhibit antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and skin problems.
